outputs.document.md:
ADSL Analysis Variable Derivation Chain
================================================

1. Treatment Variable Derivation Chain
---------------------------------------
- Source: DM.ARMCD (Character) and DM.ARM (Character)
- TRT01PN (Numeric Planned Treatment for Period 01):
  - If DM.ARMCD = "SCRNFAIL" or "NOTASSGN" then TRT01PN = 0
  - Else if DM.ARMCD = "PLACEBO" then TRT01PN = 1
  - Else if DM.ARMCD = "TRT_LOW" then TRT01PN = 2
  - Else if DM.ARMCD = "TRT_HIGH" then TRT01PN = 3
  - Else TRT01PN = . (missing)
- TRT01P (Character Planned Treatment for Period 01):
  - Derived from TRT01PN:
    - TRT01PN = 0 → TRT01P = "Screen Failure" or "Not Assigned"
    - TRT01PN = 1 → TRT01P = "Placebo"
    - TRT01PN = 2 → TRT01P = "Low Dose"
    - TRT01PN = 3 → TRT01P = "High Dose"
  - Alternatively, directly mapped from DM.ARM when DM.ARMCD is valid.
- Note: TRT01P/TRT01PN represent planned treatment for the first treatment period (typically the entire study for parallel designs). For crossover studies, additional variables (TRT02P, etc.) may be derived similarly.

2. Population Flag Criteria
----------------------------
- SAFFL (Safety Population Flag):
  - Criteria: Subject received at least one dose of study drug.
  - Derivation: If EX.EXDOSE > 0 for any record, then SAFFL = "Y"; else "N".
- ITTFL (Intent-to-Treat Population Flag):
  - Criteria: Subject was randomized (DM.ARMCD not in {"SCRNFAIL", "NOTASSGN"}).
  - Derivation: If DM.ARMCD is not missing and not "SCRNFAIL" or "NOTASSGN", then ITTFL = "Y"; else "N".
- PPFL (Per-Protocol Population Flag):
  - Criteria: Subject completed the study without major protocol deviations.
  - Derivation: Based on DV (Deviations) data. If no major deviation flagged (DV.DVMAJ = "N" or missing) and subject completed (EOSSTT = "COMPLETED"), then PPFL = "Y"; else "N".
- EFFL (Efficacy Population Flag):
  - Criteria: Subject is in ITT and has at least one post-baseline efficacy assessment.
  - Derivation: If ITTFL = "Y" and exists at least one post-baseline record in ADQS (or relevant efficacy domain) with ADY > 0, then EFFL = "Y"; else "N".

3. Baseline Measure Sources
----------------------------
- Baseline measures are typically the last non-missing measurement prior to the first dose of study drug.
- Source variables:
  - For vital signs: VS.VSSTRESN where VS.VISITNUM = baseline visit (e.g., VISITNUM = 1 or VSDTC < EX.EXSTDTC).
  - For laboratory tests: LB.LBSTRESN with similar timing.
  - For questionnaires: QS.QSSTRESN.
- Derivation rule:
  - ABLFL = "Y" for the record that is the last observation before first treatment date (EX.EXSTDTC).
  - If multiple records on same date, take the last one by time (if available) or average.
  - If no pre-dose record, baseline may be set to first post-dose value (with flag ABLFL = "Y" and warning).
- Specific ADSL baseline variables (e.g., BASE, BASECAT) are populated from the corresponding baseline record in the source domain.

4. Disposition Flag Logic
--------------------------
- DCDECOD (Disposition Code):
  - Source: DS.DSDECOD where DS.DSSCAT = "DISPOSITION EVENT" or similar.
  - If DS.DSTERM = "COMPLETED" then DCDECOD = "COMPLETED".
  - Else if DS.DSTERM contains "ADVERSE EVENT" then DCDECOD = "ADVERSE EVENT".
  - Else if DS.DSTERM contains "WITHDRAWAL BY SUBJECT" then DCDECOD = "WITHDRAWAL BY SUBJECT".
  - Else if DS.DSTERM contains "LOST TO FOLLOW-UP" then DCDECOD = "LOST TO FOLLOW-UP".
  - Else if DS.DSTERM contains "PROTOCOL VIOLATION" then DCDECOD = "PROTOCOL VIOLATION".
  - Else DCDECOD = "OTHER".
- DCREASCD (Disposition Reason):
  - Source: DS.DSREAS where DS.DSTERM is the primary disposition.
  - If DCDECOD = "ADVERSE EVENT", then DCREASCD = DS.DSREAS (e.g., specific AE term).
  - If DCDECOD = "WITHDRAWAL BY SUBJECT", then DCREASCD = DS.DSREAS (e.g., "Personal reasons").
  - Otherwise, DCREASCD = DS.DSREAS or missing.
- EOSSTT (End of Study Status):
  - If DCDECOD = "COMPLETED" then EOSSTT = "COMPLETED".
  - Else if DCDECOD is any other value then EOSSTT = "DISCONTINUED".
  - Else if subject is ongoing (no DS record) then EOSSTT = "ONGOING".
- EOTSTT (End of Treatment Status):
  - Similar logic based on treatment discontinuation records (DS.DSDECOD where DS.DSSCAT = "TREATMENT DISCONTINUATION").
  - If no treatment discontinuation record and subject completed treatment, then EOTSTT = "COMPLETED".
  - If treatment discontinued early, then EOTSTT = "DISCONTINUED".

End of Document
